data.acceptance:
# Acceptance criteria

## AC-1 — Live app: Codex row reads "Codex GPT 5.5" everywhere

Open the running app. For any beat where a Codex/GPT agent is
working:
- Beats table: Agent="Codex", Model="GPT", Version="5.5"
- Title-bar pill: "Codex GPT 5.5"
- Both reflect the same canonical form. No "gpt" lowercase
  appears in any user-visible surface for new claims.

## AC-2 — Live app: OpenCode pill reads "OpenCode OpenRouter MoonshotAI Kimi-k 2.6"

For an OpenCode session running `openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6`:
- Title-bar pill: `OpenCode OpenRouter MoonshotAI Kimi-k 2.6`
- Beats table: appropriate fields rendered with the same
  capitalisation
- No `"openrouter 2.6"` tail. No duplication of the router segment.

## AC-3 — Single-form throughout

The codebase no longer carries parallel machine and display forms
of model names:
- `MODEL_LABELS` map is removed OR retained only for one-time
  legacy migration in a clearly-named function.
- `formatModelDisplay` is either removed OR is the identity
  function with a comment explaining it is preserved only as a
  named call site for backwards compatibility.
- Per-provider extractors return display-form values
  (`"GPT"`, `"Claude"`, `"Opus"`, `"Pro"`, etc.).

Verifiable: `grep -rn '"gpt"\|"claude"\|"opus"\|"sonnet"\|"haiku"\|"chatgpt"\|"gemini"\|"pro"\|"flash"' src/lib/agent-identity*.ts | grep -v "// legacy"`
returns only inputs / regex patterns, not output values.

## AC-4 — Per-provider extractor unit tests updated

`src/lib/__tests__/agent-identity*.test.ts` cases assert the new
display-form outputs:
- Claude: `{ provider: "Claude", model: "Claude", flavor: "Opus",
  version: "4.7" }` for `claude-opus-4-7`
- Codex: `{ provider: "Codex", model: "GPT", flavor: undefined or
  "Codex Mini", version: "5.5" }` for `gpt-5.5` / `codex-mini`
- Gemini: `{ provider: "Gemini", model: "Gemini", flavor: "Pro",
  version: "2.5" }` for `gemini-2.5-pro`
- OpenCode: appropriate display-form output per the segment-
  capitalisation rules

## AC-5 — `formatAgentFamily` simplifies

The Codex special-case (`if (provider === "Codex" && model === "GPT")`)
is removed. The function reduces to a single-line filter+join:

```ts
return [provider, model, flavor].filter(Boolean).join(" ");
```

(Or equivalent — single rule, no per-provider branching.)

## AC-6 — OpenCode segment formatter

A function (likely `formatOpenCodePathSegments` or absorbed into
`normalizeOpenCodeModel`) handles:
- `openrouter` → `OpenRouter`
- `moonshotai` → `MoonshotAI`
- `kimi-k2.6` → `Kimi-k 2.6` (version split)
- `glm-5.1` → `Glm 5.1`
- `claude-sonnet-4-5` → `Claude Sonnet 4.5`
- `devstral-2512` → `Devstral 2512`

Tested in `src/lib/__tests__/agent-identity-opencode.test.ts` and
the round-trip test from foolery-cd2e.

## AC-7 — Settings migration

Existing `settings.toml` files containing machine-form agent
entries (from foolery-c4a6's canonicalisation) auto-migrate to
display-form on first read. Idempotent. Preserves user-supplied
fields (`command`, `approvalMode`, etc.). Tested.

## AC-8 — Quality gates green

`bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`,
`bun run build` all pass on `origin/main` after the change lands.

## AC-9 — Visual verification before declaring shipped

Before advancing the knot to `shipped`, the worker takes a
screenshot of the running beat table AND a running session pill,
attaches both as notes on the knot, and confirms AC-1 and AC-2 are
met visually. Do not ship on test-pass alone.

## AC-10 — Contract doc updated

`docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md` gains a section "Single
canonical form" explicitly stating that machine-form vs
display-form duality is forbidden, with the new self-audit grep:

```bash
grep -rn 'MODEL_LABELS\[' src/ | grep -v "// legacy migration"
```

returns zero hits.
